The '''Battle of Fort Nowhere''' took place one year after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].

==Background==
[[Fort Nowhere]] was a [[Class Two]] military installment functioning as a city and a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler's]] haven, on the forgotten [[planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]]; as such, it was inhabited primarily by smugglers and later dissident colonists from [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]], although most of the latter settled the badlands outside the town.<ref name="Dark Forces: Rebel Agent">''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''</ref>

The two sides in the battle were [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec's]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] branch—who were searching for the [[Valley of the Jedi]] situated not far from the fort—and the local settlers of Fort Nowhere.

==Before the battle==
[[File:Jerec_Sariss_Ruusan.jpg|thumb|left|[[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] and [[Sariss]] contemplate a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] of [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] before attacking.]]
{{Quote|Take Yun and Boc. See what you can learn. Report to me.|Jerec, to Sariss|<ref name="JK" />}}
The [[Vengeance Battle Group]] arrived above Ruusan, disabling the weather [[Satellite/Legends|satellites]] operated by the smugglers, and surveyed the unknown planet with [[probe droid/Legends|probe droids]]. Sulonese [[Grif Grawley]] witnessed it and tried to warn the other colonists.<ref name="JK" />

[[Sariss]] considered the settlement expendable and was ready to destroy it, but Jerec preferred to interview the settlers if they knew anything about the Valley. Imperial representatives [[Sariss]], [[Boc Aseca|Boc]], and [[Yun]] parlayed with [[Mayor/Legends|Mayor]] [[Byron Devo III]] of Fort Nowhere. The settlers initially being very hostile, Sariss nearly [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Grip|Force-choked]] a settler named [[Lasko]] to death to "soften up" the dwellers. The [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] drained Mayor Devo, [[Marie Peeno]], and the [[Tapcafe/Legends|tapcafe's]] owner for relevant information and promised to leave Fort Nowhere alone despite the fact that, according to Imperial law, virtually everything about Fort Nowhere was illegal.<ref name="JK" />

Confident that the settlers knew nothing about the Valley, and therefore were useless, the [[Seven Dark Jedi]] proceeded with the town's destruction, despite Devo's cooperation and Sariss's promises. They chose an enclosed valley for a staging area, where, for more than twenty-six [[standard hour/Legends|hours]], they set up a temporary maintenance facility where they buried [[fuel/Legends|fuel bladders]]. Its perimeter was patrolled by a pair of [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]]s and heavily armed troopers. The best of the Vengeance Battle Group were chosen to man the TIEs and the shuttles, focusing on speed and surprise.<ref name="JK" />

Meanwhile the settlers made use of what they had; volunteers with [[electrobinoculars/Legends|electrobinoculars]] acted as the target-acquisition system, and Peeno directed the defenses from an underground bunker, with a teenager acting as weapons-control officer.<ref name="JK" />

==The battle==
{{Quote|I concur. Destroy the settlement.|Jerec, to Sariss|<ref name="JK" />}}
The Imperial assault was primarily ground-based; the Imperial forces consisted of six [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] and four [[Assault shuttle/Legends|assault shuttles]] worth of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] [[platoon/Legends|platoons]] (that is, four times forty stormtroopers in all), staged twenty-six hours prior to the battle on the nearby flats.<ref name="JK" />

The TIE Fighters commenced the attack, destroying large portions of the Fort's defenses—including the southern gate, large portions of the walls and a storage shed—with laser fire. One of the TIEs was shot down by settler [[Dinko (Human)|Dinko]] who manned one of the [[blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannon]] turrets.<ref name="JK" /> Four [[gunboat/Legends|gunboats]] were on their way. The Alpha Wave of TIEs managed to destroy two of the defensive turrents and tried to evade the heave fire from the other four. Missiles destroyed half of the Beta Wave before it could launch.<ref name="JK audio" />

The assault shuttles followed, and a flight of six surface-to-air [[missile/Legends|missiles]] was launched against them, which however were neutralized by [[Chaff/Legends|chaff]]; one of them managed to down Imperial assault shuttle number three.<ref name="JK" /> The shuttles then locked on the Fort's missile silos.<ref name="JK audio">[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' audio drama]]</ref>

Three of the turrets had been destroyed by the time the remaining three shuttles arrived to the settlement. One TIE flew low to mow some fugitives who attempted to flee to the caves. The shuttles landed amidst the chaos, and soon the smugglers and settlers were overrun by stormtroopers.<ref name="JK" /> [[Squad/Legends|Squad]] Two secured the main road while Squad Three secured the power station.<ref name="JK audio" /> 

The Fort Nowhere forces were no match for the Imperials and the battle lasted only fifteen minutes to subdue the fort itself. The three Dark Jedi participated actively in the battle. Sariss personally beheaded Mayor Devo during the battle, while Boc amused himself by finishing off wounded smugglers and settlers. Yun was ordered to destroy the fort's subsurface farms. It took another twenty minutes to clear out its underground system of caves and tunnels.<ref name="JK" />

==Aftermath==
[[File:Moldy_Crow_Nowhere_ruins.jpg|thumb|right|150px|''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]'' flies over the ruins of [[Fort Nowhere]].]]
{{Quote|Those fortunate enough to survive the attack on Fort Nowhere banded together, collected what resources they could, and came here.|[[Grif Grawley]], to Kyle Katarn and Jan Ors|<ref name="JK" />}}
Fort Nowhere was completely razed and most of its inhabitants were killed; only very few managed to escape, most of whom were later hunted down by trainee stormtroopers. Apart from the downed TIE Fighter and assault shuttle, Imperial losses were minimal.

Some survivors retreated to the badlands, and reached Grif Grawley's place; as they acknowledged theri mistake not believing Grif's warning, and since most of them didn't know the parts outside the Fort, they saught his help. He agreed to help them, and led them to [[Unidentified Ruusan temple|the ruins of a Jedi temple]] fifty-six [[kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] south of the settlement;<ref name="JK" /> Grif Grawley transmitted a message in a [[Primitive binary code]] in a loop pointing to their location, possibly in an attempt to attract more survivors.<ref name="JK" /><ref name="JK audio" />

Some days after the battle, when the Imperials had proceeded to the Valley of the Jedi, [[Kyle Katarn]] and [[Jan Ors]] arrived at what was once Fort Nowhere in the ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]''. Shocked with the devastation, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agents searched in vain for survivors among the ruins. Following Grif's signal, they reached the temple and joined the survivors, who helped them find the Valley of the Jedi.<ref name="JK" />

==Behind the scenes==
The narrative of ''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' mentions "TIE fighters", six of them, participating in the battle. However the accompanying illustration (see main image) by [[William C. Dietz]] shows six [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE '''bombers''']] flying above.

The [[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|audio drama]] suggests a larger imperial force of "couple of dozens" TIEs, as well as hovercraft and gunboats. In the novella Marie Peeno is in a bunker while Dinko is manning a cannon turret, whereas in the audio drama they are together, and die together while Peeno is arming some missiles.
